articles.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
articles.summary: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

articles.title: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
articles.summary: Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.

articles.title: Trump Says Iran Agreed to Allow 20 More Ships of Oil Through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The president, speaking to reporters on Air Force One, cast the permission as a “sign of respect.” He also asserted that the United States had already achieved “regime change” in Iran.

articles.title: ICE officers could remain at airports after TSA workers are paid
articles.summary: Even when Transportation Security Administration workers get paid, Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents could still be present at U.S. airports.

articles.title: New U.S. Missile Hit Iranian Sports Hall and School, Analysis Shows
articles.summary: The Pentagon used missiles untested in combat in a deadly attack that struck civilian sites near a military compound on Feb. 28, according to visual evidence examined by The Times and weapons experts.

articles.title: U.S. Allows Russian Oil Tanker to Reach Cuba, Despite Blockade
articles.summary: The tanker full of crude oil could reach its expected destination by Monday, providing a lifeline to the island amid intense U.S. pressure.

articles.title: The Final Four is set with UConn stunning Duke to join Illinois, Arizona and Michigan
articles.summary: The Huskies beat Duke with a 3-pointer from the logo with 0.4 seconds left by Braylon Mullins, who grew up just outside of Indianapolis.

articles.title: How young people are navigating a tough and rapidly changing job market
articles.summary: Finding a job is hard right now, especially for young people starting their careers. NPR's Adrian Ma spoke with college students and an economist about navigating today's tough job market.

articles.title: Understanding why some Iranian Americans support the war on their country of origin
articles.summary: Protesters from the Iranian diaspora in the U.S. gathered in Washington, D.C. as the war in the Middle East broadens. Many say they are aligned with the U.S. and Israel and explain why they want to se

articles.title: For NASA’s Artemis II Crew, Journey to the Moon ‘Starting to Feel Real’
articles.summary: The four astronauts — three Americans and one Canadian — spoke from a prelaunch quarantine ahead of their scheduled Wednesday mission.

articles.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
articles.summary: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

articles.title: New Political Group to Push Trump’s A.I. Agenda in Midterms
articles.summary: The group, Innovation Council Action, says it plans to spend at least $100 million. It will be led by a former administration official.

articles.title: ICE May Remain at Airports Even After T.S.A. Pay Resumes, Border Czar Says
articles.summary: Transportation safety officers are set to be paid on Monday, but Tom Homan, the White House’s border czar, said ICE agents may stay where there are shortages.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

articles.title: How to navigate the maze of drug discounts to get the best price
articles.summary: In February, TrumpRx joined a growing list of websites consumers can tap for discounts on their medicines. Here's a cheat sheet for getting the best deal.

articles.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
articles.summary: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

articles.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
articles.summary: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

articles.title: Castro Heirs Emerge Across Cuba’s Political Scene Amid Energy Crisis and Trump Threats
articles.summary: As Trump officials demand changes, Castro family members are suddenly popping up across Cuba’s political scene. Some even ask: Could one be the “Cuban Delcy?”

articles.title: The iconic South African theater that took on apartheid
articles.summary: South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

articles.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
articles.summary: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
